clusters.title: Heartbeat-delivered sampler config has no regression test

member_findings.title: Heartbeat-delivered sampler config has no regression test
member_findings.impact: The new client heartbeat path applies LogSampleConfigPb updates after registration, but the added ST only verifies the initial register-delivered snapshot. If heartbeat responses stop carrying or applying updated sampling config, long-lived clients will keep stale sampler state and this PR's tests would still pass.
member_findings.fix: Add a test that connects a client, changes or injects a heartbeat LogSampleConfigPb with different ppm values, waits for the heartbeat path, and asserts the client LogSampler snapshot changes; cover both missing-field keeps-current and invalid-field retains-previous-good behavior if practical.

clusters.title: New EmbeddedConfig sampler setters are untested

member_findings.title: New EmbeddedConfig sampler setters are untested
member_findings.impact: The PR adds public embedded-worker configuration setters that silently accept valid rates and ignore invalid, NaN, or out-of-range values, but the diff adds no test that these setters emit the expected request_sample_rate/access_sample_rate/diagnostic_sample_rate args or preserve prior config on invalid input. A regression here would leave embedded deployments unable to configure the new sampler or accidentally accept bad rates without a failing test.
member_findings.fix: Add focused EmbeddedConfig tests covering all three new setters, chaining behavior, exact generated arg names/values, boundary values 0.0 and 1.0, and rejection of negative, >1.0, NaN, and infinity inputs.

clusters.title: Default access logging now allocates and copies an extra formatted request string

member_findings.title: Default access logging now allocates and copies an extra formatted request string
member_findings.impact: When the sampler is disabled or all rates fold to pass-through, IsCurrentRequestLogSampled() returns true for every request trace, so LogPerformance() calls FormatAccessReqMsg(), builds a separate ostringstream/string, appends logSampled:true, then copies that result into the outer logStream for every access record. This adds an extra allocation/copy and increases emitted access-log payload on the default hot access-log path.
member_findings.fix: Keep the pass-through path as a direct append: either return false when the sampler is disabled unless an actual admitted decision exists, or change FormatAccessReqMsg() back to append into the caller's stream and only allocate/insert the marker when a real sampled-in decision needs it.

clusters.title: Repeated protobuf keys are copied into a vector before formatting

member_findings.title: Repeated protobuf keys are copied into a vector before formatting
member_findings.impact: PROTO_REF access records now allocate a std::vector and copy every protobuf key string before calling objectKeysToString(keys). Worker batch paths such as Exist/DeleteAllCopy/MultiCreate pass RepeatedPtrField values through this path, so sampled-in access logs pay an avoidable O(n) allocation and string-copy cost on batch request handling.
member_findings.fix: Use the new objectKeysToString(const google::protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<std::string>&) overload directly from the PROTO_REF branch, or add a summary variant for protobuf fields when only abbreviated output is needed.

clusters.title: Snapshot publication logic is duplicated across config paths

member_findings.title: Snapshot publication logic is duplicated across config paths
member_findings.impact: The proto update path manually rebuilds SampleRate objects, allocates a LogSamplerSnapshot, exchanges the atomic pointer, retires the old snapshot, and stores samplerEnabled. The flag path in log_sampler.cpp has a second hand-written version of the same publication sequence. Future changes to snapshot ownership, added sampling dimensions, or memory-ordering rules will need to be mirrored in both places, making this central concurrency-adjacent configuration code easy to drift.
member_findings.fix: Extract a shared helper that takes an effective LogSampleConfig or ppm triple, builds thresholds, publishes the snapshot, retires the old snapshot, and updates samplerEnabled. Have both UpdateConfigFromProto() and BuildAndPublishSnapshot() use that helper.
